original text:
Academic advising plays an important role for all students at Illinois Tech. The few minutes it takes to review course choices and discuss scheduling options with an adviser helps ensure all courses taken will apply towards the student's degree program.
Graduate students are required to consult with an academic advisor on course choices in order to obtain the Advising PIN.
student enrollment at Illinois Tech.
New On-Campus Graduate Students will receive their PIN after completing an Advising and Registration session held during the week prior to courses starting. Students will receive email communication on how to sign up for these sessions. If you have not received such communication, please email Renee McBrien at mcbrienr@iit.edu in the FdSN Department for instructions.
Online, Certificate, and Non-Degree Graduate Students must consult with their Advisor, Renee McBrien at mcbrienr@iit.edu, to receive their PIN and discuss course choices.
Once the PIN is issued and used for registration, it will appear in the IIT personal ID channel in myIIT portal.
FdSN students will be emailed by their advisor on how to schedule an Advising Appointment.

original text:
Each year 250+ Utah secondary and college students participating in the Youthlinc Service Year contribute 22,000+ hours of local service, becoming relied upon volunteers at service sites all over the state. This is the largest - and most long lasting - part of their commitment to participate in the Youthlinc Service Year.
Before they can implement the international service planned in their monthly team meetings, each of these students must contribute 80 hours of local service. Minimally 40 hours of these hours must be volunteered at one location - their main service site.
Encourage these students - who must volunteer 80 hours - to volunteer with you!
If your organization is already in the Local Service Directory, that means a student recommended your organization to us. You are a Bronze Partner!
The first step to become a Silver or Gold Partner is to attend our annual Volunteer Management Training. Click here to read what last year's attendees had to say about the training.
To register, click here or contact Youthlinc Local Service Director, Shannon Moss, at shannon@youthlinc.org or 801-467-4417 ext 3 by Friday, October 5th.
WHY A YOUTHLINC VOLUNTEER MANAGEMENT TRAINING?
Since our inception in 1999, we've mentored 2,500 students through 200,000 hours of Utah service. We've taught 600+ adult professionals how to mentor students.
Our proven strategies of student leadership, hands-on service, and mentoring allow our students to create an emotional bond with those whom they serve and develop a deep-rooted service ethic, which they carry throughout a lifetime.
Our Local Service Director has organized and led successful service initiatives over the past 15 years.
Currently she oversees the 22,000 hours of meaningful service contributed by our Youthlinc Service Year students each year.
As an expert in youth volunteerism, Youthlinc wants to share our knowledge & experience with you so that together, we help instill a service ethic in Utah's greatest resource-our youth.
We want to thank Chik-fil-A Sugarhouse our sponsor of the 2018 Volunteer Management Training. They have generously donated our lunch for this event. THANK YOU!

original text:
Can we tempt you with a taster?
Thinking of coming on a case workshop but not sure if it's right for you? Perhaps you've heard about the case method, but don't know much about it? Or maybe you're looking for new ways to enthuse and inspire your students. If so, one of our professional development workshops (PDWs) will be perfect for you.
We hold PDWs on the case method throughout the year at various conferences and events. Led by one of our highly experienced case method tutors, these taster sessions are the ideal way to find out more. They're mostly under two hours long and usually free for conference delegates to attend - we're sure you'll have fun, learn a lot, and be inspired to explore further.
We expect to be running PDWs at the following events and will confirm details as soon as we have them. Watch this space!
The Case Centre is pleased to be hosting a PDW, led by Trevor Williamson, at the Chartered Association of Business Schools (CABS) Learning, Teaching & Student Experience (LTSE) conference in Manchester in May.
Want to discover new tips on how to get the most out of your case writings skills? Trevor will be able to help during this three-hour workshop.
For further details, please contact Oliver Lowe at oliver.lowe@charteredabs.org.
If you're new to case teaching or need to brush up your skills, why not come on a workshop or ask about our customised workshops? You can double up and organise a complementary Student Session to introduce your students to the excitement and exhilaration of case learning.

Use this form to request a quote for OnePager software. After completing the form, a quote will be immediately e-mailed to you.
Enterprise site-licensing is also available. Contact sales@onepager.com for more information.
Your quote will be e-mailed to the address you provide below. Please include your billing address so that sales tax can be correctly calculated.
Once purchased, all items quoted fall under our standard terms and conditions.
